vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with visual reality. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or lost. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, anchored by the white rabbit figurine.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Pothos leaf visible, rabbit anchor present)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No visible silver guest foliage)

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil; no String of Nickels detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains debris and soil; no viable plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf remains in the black pot with the white rabbit; shows signs of chlorosis or low-light stress.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.anomalies: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P3/P4 area).
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a white cable/wire introduced in later frames.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Minimal change; the single Pothos leaf remains static. The eggshell fragments remain in the same position.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Significant drop in ambient light levels, obscuring detail; the scene appears to have transitioned to a low-light or night-mode state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. With the exception of one Pothos leaf, the registered specimens are effectively lost. The lack of growth and the presence of debris suggest a neglected environment.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing.
White cable/wire intrusion near the yellow pot.
Significant light level fluctuation between T-2 and CURRENT.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the provided images. My first step was to establish the baseline registry against the visual data, which revealed a high rate of attrition. I then validated these findings by tracking the specific coordinates of the white rabbit anchor and the remaining Pothos leaf. The biome is currently failing to support the registered species, with P1, P2, and P4 showing no signs of life. The environment is static, suggesting a lack of active horticultural maintenance.
